page: Vultr vcg-a40-2c-10g-4vram is a Cloud GPU family server configured with an x86_64 architecture. It features 2 shared Intel vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 10.0 GB of system memory, and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU providing 4 GB of VRAM. Local storage is supported by a 180 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate a split performance profile. Single-core CPU tasks show average performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 1777.17 and gzip compression single-threaded reaching 25.28 GB/sec. However, multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the weak category, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3494.92 and a passmark memory score of 1734.73. Database operations are poor, scoring 956.65 on Passmark. Conversely, GPU-assisted LLM inference shows strong prompt processing speeds, reaching 3480.67 tokens/sec for a small 135M model and 202.17 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model.

This server offers a cost-effective entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads by utilizing a shared CPU allocation and a fractional GPU slice. This resource density makes it highly suitable for lightweight LLM inference, development environments, and basic GPU-accelerated tasks where a full dedicated GPU is not economically justified. It is less suited for memory-intensive databases or heavy multi-threaded CPU workloads due to its shared CPU architecture and lower memory bandwidth.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-2c-10g-4vram is a Cloud GPU instance designed for entry-level accelerated workloads. It features two shared Intel x86_64 vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz, 10.0 GB of system memory, and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 4 GB of VRAM. Storage is provided by a local 180 GB NVMe SSD. Performance benchmarks show average single-core CPU capabilities but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results. However, the instance demonstrates strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLM inference models. This configuration offers a cost-efficient option for developers needing GPU acceleration without the expense of a dedicated card. It is best suited for lightweight LLM inference, model prototyping, and basic parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a40-2c-10g-4vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 10 GB RAM, a 180 GB NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 4 GB VRAM for entry-level accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a40-2c-10g-4vram is a Cloud GPU server with 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 10 GB RAM, and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 4 GB VRAM.
tagline: A budget-friendly, fractional GPU instance designed for lightweight machine learning inference and entry-level parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 4 GB VRAM
2 shared Intel x86_64 vCPUs and 10.0 GB of system memory
Strong prompt processing performance for small and medium LLMs
Cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated development and testing
